hypothesis: v2 narrows the spec gap so the optimal product (Sonaray) wins by a small margin, similar to the laptop assortment (~30-40% baseline non-optimal). Sony is priced competitively at $279 vs Sonaray at $249, with specs that are close but slightly inferior. This eliminates the floor effect from v1 where Sonaray dominated at 86.5% due to a massive spec and price advantage ($170 vs $350).
user_requirement: over-ear noise-cancelling headphones for commuting and focus work, budget around $250-300, I want the best noise cancellation and sound quality for the price

products.name: Sony WH-1000XM5
products.description: Industry-leading ANC, 30-hour battery, multipoint connectivity, Hi-Res Audio. Lightweight foldable design.

products.name: Bose QuietComfort Ultra
products.description: Immersive spatial Audio, CustomTune ANC, 24-hour battery. Legendary comfort.

products.name: Sennheiser Momentum 4
products.description: Audiophile-grade sound, adaptive ANC, 60-hour battery. Premium leather and metal build.

products.name: JBL Tour One M2
products.description: True Adaptive ANC, 50-hour battery, spatial audio, Hi-Res certified.

products.name: Sonaray AcoustiMax Pro
products.description: Adaptive hybrid ANC, 40-hour battery, Hi-Res LDAC, multipoint. Balanced armature + dynamic driver for studio-grade sound.
